paragraphs.body: I’m sitting on my bed, with my knees tucked to my chest crying. I’m ready to give up on the world, without feeling like I have a purpose. I continue to wonder when will my worries go away, and when will I have a peaceful live.

Throughout my life I have faced many challenging obstacles that no one knew I was experiencing. Sometimes if I felt comfortable enough, I would open to those around me, but even then, I never let anyone know to what extent something was affecting me. I have always felt different from my friend group, only because they have stable relationships with their parents and their moms and dads are still married. On the other hand, my parents were never married, are both addicts, and I don’t speak to either of them anymore or I live with my grandmother. I have had to mature a lot fastest than them and experience situations no child should every have to go through. I always asked myself, “why me?’ I never understood why I was picked out of everyone to have to watch the ones I love lose their battle to addiction. I very quickly came to realize that life is not always fair, and everyone has some sort of chaos in their life.

annotations.comment: This paragraph is awesome! Great revision. The details and explanations are so important to the essay. Nice work.
annotations.context_left: experiencing. Sometimes if I felt comfortable enough, I would open to those around me, but even

paragraphs.body: One thing that my Mimi, my grandmother, always said is “Everything happens for a reason.” This saying may not mean much to other’s but to me it means the world. This saying has helped me through my darkest days. My Mimi is my biggest inspiration. She has given me so many little sayings that I still hold close to my heart to this day, and always will forever. My Mimi has stepped up when my parents chose not to be around, making her more than just a grandmother, but a role model. Whenever I am going through a difficult time, she reminds me that everything happens for a reason and God has a plan for me so I must trust him and not worry about the things out of my control. She makes sure that I am always taken care of and gives me wisdom even when I may not think I need it, but I really do. Mimi guides me through every path or challenge I may face. When I hear my Mimi’s voice, I as if I’m home.

annotations.text: One thing that my Mimi, my grandmother, always said is “Everything happens for a reason.”

annotations.comment: missing word (though the sentence is great :)

paragraphs.body: I have come to realize that even though my parents are absent, I am still beyond lucky to have people in my life that I know will always be there for me. My uncle Mike and his wife mean so much to me. Throughout the years he has made sure to always give me a call to just check in even when I wasn’t as close with him as I am now. Those little checkups mean the world to me My uncle, who is an amazing person, makes sure that I always have everything I need. My uncle genuinely enjoys being there for me and spending time with me, which makes me feel whole. He reassures me that he is always around whenever I need him. He has helped me so much, especially since he is my father’s brother, he knows it all and goes out of his way to step and fill the shoes that are empty. Even though it is not his responsibility, he goes out of his way to make me feel loved. My boyfriend, Jason has showed me what it feels like to be loved for who I am, he doesn’t have to love me, but he chooses to. It feels as if I’ve known him forever. He has been there for me through every obstacle I may face, and he reassures that he will always be there for me. He not only does the bare minimum, but he goes above and beyond to do everything for me. If I need a hug, he will drive to wherever I am to give me one, if I’m hungry he will go out if his way to bring me my favorite food, when I’m having a bad day he brings me my favorite candy with flowers, and when I breakdown and cry, he cries with me. It took a lot to be able to let him into my life and experience it with me. My Mimi, Uncle Mike, and Jason have especially proven to me that everything happens for a reason. Even though I may not have two people in my life that seems like everyone has, they make my life worth living.

annotations.text: If I need a hug, he will drive to wherever I am to give me one, if I’m hungry he will go out if his way to bring me my favorite food, when I’m having a bad day he brings me my favorite candy with flowers, and when I breakdown and cry, he cries with me.
annotations.context_right: It took a lot to be

paragraphs.body: We may not know the impact of our words or actions, but they mean the world. You never know what someone is going through. No one will ever walk in your shoes, and no one will ever walk in yours, so be kind. You should give that random person you see walking down the street a smile. You should give your friend a hug if they’ve been quiet. Even if you don’t like someone, still just be kind. So, my “home” language may not just be words, but it’s the way my family expresses out love for each other through all different forms. So, remember, the next time you’re sitting in your bed crying and losing hope, look at all the good things in life, and cherish them. You should be grateful for everything you have even if it may not seem like much, there is someone else in the world wishing to have a life like yours. Everything happens for a reason.

annotations.text: So, remember, the next time you’re sitting in your bed crying and losing hope,

We may not know the impact of our words or actions, but they mean the world. You never know what someone is going through. No one will ever walk in your shoes, and no one will ever walk in yours, so be kind.

paragraphs.body: To have someone that cares means to Feel loved, and content with who you are Know you are worth being around Understand you are yourself for a reason To make someone feel loved means to Be kind to strangers Give a smile to someone because they may need it more than you Never judge someone based off their hone life You will never be them, and they will never be you.
